title: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 2 - Saving the Vaquita
rawPassageText:
READING PASSAGE 2
You should spend about 20 minutes on Questions 14-26, which are based on Reading Passage 2 below.

Saving the Vaquita

A

In 2026, conservationists working in Mexico's Upper Gulf of California are attempting to prevent the disappearance of the vaquita, the world's smallest and rarest porpoise. The animal is not hunted directly, but it becomes trapped in gillnets set for fish and shrimp, especially for totoaba, a large fish whose dried swim bladder is sold illegally at high prices. The demand for this product has risen rapidly in some black-market luxury and traditional medicine networks, making enforcement unusually difficult. A few vaquitas are still detected by visual surveys and underwater microphones, and the aim is no longer to design a perfect rescue plan in the future. It is to keep every remaining animal alive long enough for safer fishing to replace gillnets in its habitat.

B

The vaquita's range is remarkably small. Unlike many whales and dolphins, it does not migrate across oceans but lives in a limited part of the northern Gulf of California. Researchers usually describe its remaining core habitat as an area of only a few thousand square kilometres, a tiny space compared with the routes used by most marine mammals. This limited range makes protection possible in theory: patrol boats, acoustic sensors and fishing-gear inspections can be concentrated in one region. Yet it also means that a single illegal net can do disproportionate damage. A conservation measure that works on paper can fail if it leaves even small gaps in the area where vaquitas still feed and breed.

C

The danger is partly explained by the way a gillnet is formed. A curtain of fine mesh is suspended in the water by floats at the top and weights at the bottom, creating a nearly invisible wall. Fish swim into the mesh and are caught by their gills, but air-breathing animals can also become entangled when they pass through the same fishing grounds. In the Upper Gulf, this means that commercial species and vaquitas may co-exist in the same shallow waters, while the net itself makes no distinction between a target catch and a protected animal. For this reason, many scientists argue that removing gillnets is more important than moving vaquitas elsewhere.

D

The 2026 preservation attempt combines old and new methods. Crews remove abandoned nets, test vaquita-safe gear and use acoustic equipment to listen for the high-frequency clicks that porpoises produce when they navigate and hunt. Dr Miguel Torres, who works on acoustic monitoring, says researchers know surprisingly little about the animal's daily movements. In his view, more is known about some distant whale populations than about what a vaquita does during a single night in the Upper Gulf. Professor Hannah Blake, a conservation geneticist, adds that the species is not necessarily doomed by its small population. She argues that if deaths in nets stop, the remaining animals may still have enough genetic resilience to recover slowly.

E

The social side of the project is more difficult. Ana Rojas, Julian Chen and Farid Sadiq, who study coastal livelihoods, argue that the burden of conservation often falls on fishing families who did not create the international trade in totoaba. They say the negative effects of restrictions on local communities are too easily ignored, especially when compensation arrives late or alternative gear is expensive. Dr Laura Medina, a marine mammal ecologist, accepts that enforcement is essential, but believes a move away from patrol-only conservation is sensible. She argues that fishers need reliable vaquita-safe equipment, access to legal markets and a reason to see the animal's survival as part of their own future. Medina says trials suggest that vaquita-safe gear could catch shrimp and fish with far less bycatch than gillnets. In practice, the work often involves adapting fishing gear already tested in other small-scale fisheries.

F

Governments and conservation groups also face legal and diplomatic obstacles. Captain Elena Ruiz, who has coordinated patrol work, says no other human activity threatens the vaquita as immediately as gillnet fishing in its remaining habitat. However, the trade that drives the problem crosses borders: totoaba may be caught in Mexico, transported through several countries and sold far from the Gulf. For this reason, concerned groups argue that enforcement remains necessary because illegal nets may still enter the vaquita's habitat. Mexico, the United States and China have cooperated on enforcement, but the countries involved have yet to agree on a single set of rules for tracing, punishing and preventing the illegal trade. Even so, Blake says there is one point on which experts broadly agree: gillnets should not be used inside the vaquita's core habitat.

Questions 14-17

Reading Passage 2 has six paragraphs, A-F.

Which paragraph contains the following information?

Write the correct letter, A-F, in boxes 14-17 on your answer sheet.

14 how a particular fishing device, where target species and protected animals may co-exist, is formed
15 a rough estimate of the small area in which the animal still lives
16 reference to the rapidly increasing demand for one product in the illegal wildlife trade
17 reference to the fact that several countries have yet to agree on rules for preventing the trade that threatens the species

Questions 18-23

Look at the following statements (Questions 18-23) and the list of people below.

Match each statement with the correct person or people, A-E.

Write the correct letter, A-E, in boxes 18-23 on your answer sheet.

NB You may use any letter more than once.

18 More is known about some distant marine mammals than about the vaquita's behaviour.
19 The species may still recover if accidental deaths are stopped.
20 The negative effects of vaquita protection on local people are being overlooked.
21 A move away from conservation based only on patrols is a good idea.
22 No other form of human activity is as urgent a threat to the vaquita as gillnet fishing.
23 There is one fishing method that experts agree should not be used in the vaquita's main habitat.

List of People

A Dr Laura Medina
B Ana Rojas, Julian Chen and Farid Sadiq
C Dr Miguel Torres
D Captain Elena Ruiz
E Professor Hannah Blake

Questions 24-26

Complete the summary below.

Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.

Write your answers in boxes 24-26 on your answer sheet.

Saving the vaquita

Supporters of the preservation attempt believe that fishing in the Upper Gulf can continue if safer methods replace gillnets. They also say that vaquita-safe equipment can catch fish and shrimp without producing much 24 .................... .

The work is often done by adapting fishing 25 .................... that has already been tested in other small-scale fisheries. The method involves removing abandoned nets, listening for porpoise clicks and helping fishers use legal alternatives. However, concerned groups strongly believe that 26 .................... is necessary because illegal nets may still enter the vaquita's habitat.
rawAnswerKey:
Question 14: how a particular fishing device, where target species and protected animals may co-exist, is formed

Correct Answer: C

Accepted Answers: C

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 2 - Saving the Vaquita

Exact Portion: A curtain of fine mesh is suspended in the water by floats at the top and weights at the bottom, creating a nearly invisible wall

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ C เพราะข้อมูลที่โจทย์ถามตรงกับหลักฐานใน passage: “A curtain of fine mesh is suspended in the water by floats at the top and weights at the bottom, creating a nearly invisible wall” ย่อหน้า C อธิบายว่า gillnet ถูกสร้างอย่างไร และบอกว่า target catch กับ protected animal อาจอยู่ในพื้นที่เดียวกัน ต้องจับคู่จากความหมายรวมและผู้พูด/ย่อหน้าที่ให้ข้อมูลนี้ ไม่ใช่เลือกจากคำที่ดูคล้ายเพียงอย่างเดียว

Paraphrased Vocabulary: fishing device = gillnet; formed = suspended / creating; co-exist = in the same shallow waters; answer letter = correct paragraph/person

Question 15: a rough estimate of the small area in which the animal still lives

Correct Answer: B

Accepted Answers: B

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 2 - Saving the Vaquita

Exact Portion: Researchers usually describe its remaining core habitat as an area of only a few thousand square kilometres

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ B เพราะข้อมูลที่โจทย์ถามตรงกับหลักฐานใน passage: “Researchers usually describe its remaining core habitat as an area of only a few thousand square kilometres” rough estimate = a few thousand square kilometres และ area in which the animal still lives = remaining core habitat อยู่ในย่อหน้า B ต้องจับคู่จากความหมายรวมและผู้พูด/ย่อหน้าที่ให้ข้อมูลนี้ ไม่ใช่เลือกจากคำที่ดูคล้ายเพียงอย่างเดียว

Paraphrased Vocabulary: small area = limited range / core habitat; still lives = remaining; answer letter = correct paragraph/person

Question 16: reference to the rapidly increasing demand for one product in the illegal wildlife trade

Correct Answer: A

Accepted Answers: A

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 2 - Saving the Vaquita

Exact Portion: The demand for this product has risen rapidly in some black-market luxury and traditional medicine networks

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ A เพราะข้อมูลที่โจทย์ถามตรงกับหลักฐานใน passage: “The demand for this product has risen rapidly in some black-market luxury and traditional medicine networks” rapidly increasing demand = demand has risen rapidly และ one product หมายถึง dried swim bladder ที่ขายผิดกฎหมาย อยู่ในย่อหน้า A ต้องจับคู่จากความหมายรวมและผู้พูด/ย่อหน้าที่ให้ข้อมูลนี้ ไม่ใช่เลือกจากคำที่ดูคล้ายเพียงอย่างเดียว

Paraphrased Vocabulary: rapidly increasing demand = demand has risen rapidly; illegal wildlife trade = black-market networks; answer letter = correct paragraph/person

Question 17: reference to the fact that several countries have yet to agree on rules for preventing the trade that threatens the species

Correct Answer: F

Accepted Answers: F

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 2 - Saving the Vaquita

Exact Portion: the countries involved have yet to agree on a single set of rules for tracing, punishing and preventing the illegal trade

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ F เพราะข้อมูลที่โจทย์ถามตรงกับหลักฐานใน passage: “the countries involved have yet to agree on a single set of rules for tracing, punishing and preventing the illegal trade” several countries have yet to agree on rules = countries involved have yet to agree on a single set of rules อยู่ในย่อหน้า F ต้องจับคู่จากความหมายรวมและผู้พูด/ย่อหน้าที่ให้ข้อมูลนี้ ไม่ใช่เลือกจากคำที่ดูคล้ายเพียงอย่างเดียว

Paraphrased Vocabulary: several countries = countries involved; preventing the trade = preventing the illegal trade; answer letter = correct paragraph/person

Question 18: More is known about some distant marine mammals than about the vaquita's behaviour.

Correct Answer: C

Accepted Answers: C

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 2 - Saving the Vaquita

Exact Portion: Dr Miguel Torres, who works on acoustic monitoring, says researchers know surprisingly little about the animal's daily movements. In his view, more is known about some distant whale populations than about what a vaquita does during a single night in the Upper Gulf

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ C เพราะข้อมูลที่โจทย์ถามตรงกับหลักฐานใน passage: “Dr Miguel Torres, who works on acoustic monitoring, says researchers know surprisingly little about the animal's daily movements. In his view, more is known about some distant whale populations than about what a vaquita does during a single night in the Upper Gulf” distant marine mammals = distant whale populations และ behaviour = what a vaquita does จึงเป็น Dr Miguel Torres ต้องจับคู่จากความหมายรวมและผู้พูด/ย่อหน้าที่ให้ข้อมูลนี้ ไม่ใช่เลือกจากคำที่ดูคล้ายเพียงอย่างเดียว

Paraphrased Vocabulary: marine mammals = whale populations; behaviour = what a vaquita does; answer letter = correct paragraph/person

Question 19: The species may still recover if accidental deaths are stopped.

Correct Answer: E

Accepted Answers: E

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 2 - Saving the Vaquita

Exact Portion: if deaths in nets stop, the remaining animals may still have enough genetic resilience to recover slowly

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ E เพราะข้อมูลที่โจทย์ถามตรงกับหลักฐานใน passage: “if deaths in nets stop, the remaining animals may still have enough genetic resilience to recover slowly” may still recover = may still have enough genetic resilience to recover และ accidental deaths = deaths in nets จึงเป็น Professor Hannah Blake ต้องจับคู่จากความหมายรวมและผู้พูด/ย่อหน้าที่ให้ข้อมูลนี้ ไม่ใช่เลือกจากคำที่ดูคล้ายเพียงอย่างเดียว

Paraphrased Vocabulary: accidental deaths = deaths in nets; stopped = stop; recover = recover slowly; answer letter = correct paragraph/person

Question 20: The negative effects of vaquita protection on local people are being overlooked.

Correct Answer: B

Accepted Answers: B

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 2 - Saving the Vaquita

Exact Portion: Ana Rojas, Julian Chen and Farid Sadiq, who study coastal livelihoods, argue that the burden of conservation often falls on fishing families who did not create the international trade in totoaba. They say the negative effects of restrictions on local communities are too easily ignored

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ B เพราะข้อมูลที่โจทย์ถามตรงกับหลักฐานใน passage: “Ana Rojas, Julian Chen and Farid Sadiq, who study coastal livelihoods, argue that the burden of conservation often falls on fishing families who did not create the international trade in totoaba. They say the negative effects of restrictions on local communities are too easily ignored” local people = local communities และ overlooked = ignored จึงเป็น Ana Rojas, Julian Chen and Farid Sadiq ต้องจับคู่จากความหมายรวมและผู้พูด/ย่อหน้าที่ให้ข้อมูลนี้ ไม่ใช่เลือกจากคำที่ดูคล้ายเพียงอย่างเดียว

Paraphrased Vocabulary: vaquita protection = restrictions; overlooked = ignored; answer letter = correct paragraph/person

Question 21: A move away from conservation based only on patrols is a good idea.

Correct Answer: A

Accepted Answers: A

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 2 - Saving the Vaquita

Exact Portion: Dr Laura Medina, a marine mammal ecologist, accepts that enforcement is essential, but believes a move away from patrol-only conservation is sensible

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ A เพราะข้อมูลที่โจทย์ถามตรงกับหลักฐานใน passage: “Dr Laura Medina, a marine mammal ecologist, accepts that enforcement is essential, but believes a move away from patrol-only conservation is sensible” good idea = sensible และ conservation based only on patrols = patrol-only conservation จึงเป็น Dr Laura Medina ต้องจับคู่จากความหมายรวมและผู้พูด/ย่อหน้าที่ให้ข้อมูลนี้ ไม่ใช่เลือกจากคำที่ดูคล้ายเพียงอย่างเดียว

Paraphrased Vocabulary: based only on patrols = patrol-only; good idea = sensible; answer letter = correct paragraph/person

Question 22: No other form of human activity is as urgent a threat to the vaquita as gillnet fishing.

Correct Answer: D

Accepted Answers: D

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 2 - Saving the Vaquita

Exact Portion: Captain Elena Ruiz, who has coordinated patrol work, says no other human activity threatens the vaquita as immediately as gillnet fishing in its remaining habitat

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ D เพราะข้อมูลที่โจทย์ถามตรงกับหลักฐานใน passage: “Captain Elena Ruiz, who has coordinated patrol work, says no other human activity threatens the vaquita as immediately as gillnet fishing in its remaining habitat” as urgent a threat = threatens as immediately และ no other human activity ตรงกับ passage จึงเป็น Captain Elena Ruiz ต้องจับคู่จากความหมายรวมและผู้พูด/ย่อหน้าที่ให้ข้อมูลนี้ ไม่ใช่เลือกจากคำที่ดูคล้ายเพียงอย่างเดียว

Paraphrased Vocabulary: urgent = immediate; threat = threatens; answer letter = correct paragraph/person

Question 23: There is one fishing method that experts agree should not be used in the vaquita's main habitat.

Correct Answer: E

Accepted Answers: E

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 2 - Saving the Vaquita

Exact Portion: Blake says there is one point on which experts broadly agree: gillnets should not be used inside the vaquita's core habitat

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ E เพราะข้อมูลที่โจทย์ถามตรงกับหลักฐานใน passage: “Blake says there is one point on which experts broadly agree: gillnets should not be used inside the vaquita's core habitat” one fishing method = gillnets และ should not be used in main habitat = should not be used inside core habitat จึงเป็น Professor Hannah Blake ต้องจับคู่จากความหมายรวมและผู้พูด/ย่อหน้าที่ให้ข้อมูลนี้ ไม่ใช่เลือกจากคำที่ดูคล้ายเพียงอย่างเดียว

Paraphrased Vocabulary: main habitat = core habitat; experts agree = experts broadly agree; answer letter = correct paragraph/person

Question 24: Supporters of the preservation attempt believe that fishing in the Upper Gulf can continue if safer methods replace gillnets. They also say that vaquita-safe equipment can catch fish and shrimp without producing much .................... .

Correct Answer: bycatch

Accepted Answers: bycatch

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 2 - Saving the Vaquita

Exact Portion: vaquita-safe gear could catch shrimp and fish with far less bycatch than gillnets

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ bycatch เพราะช่องว่างต้องเติมคำจากหลักฐานใน passage: “vaquita-safe gear could catch shrimp and fish with far less bycatch than gillnets” without producing much = with far less และ unwanted catch = bycatch คำนี้พอดีกับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคและเป็นคำเดียวตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY

Paraphrased Vocabulary: equipment = gear; fish and shrimp = shrimp and fish; answer signal = bycatch

Question 25: The work is often done by adapting fishing .................... that has already been tested in other small-scale fisheries.

Correct Answer: gear

Accepted Answers: gear

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 2 - Saving the Vaquita

Exact Portion: adapting fishing gear already tested in other small-scale fisheries

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ gear เพราะช่องว่างต้องเติมคำจากหลักฐานใน passage: “adapting fishing gear already tested in other small-scale fisheries” ประโยคในข้อถอดจาก passage โดยตรง คำที่เติมคือ gear คำนี้พอดีกับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคและเป็นคำเดียวตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY

Paraphrased Vocabulary: has already been tested = already tested; answer signal = gear

Question 26: However, concerned groups strongly believe that .................... is necessary because illegal nets may still enter the vaquita's habitat.

Correct Answer: enforcement

Accepted Answers: enforcement

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 2 - Saving the Vaquita

Exact Portion: concerned groups argue that enforcement remains necessary because illegal nets may still enter the vaquita's habitat

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ enforcement เพราะช่องว่างต้องเติมคำจากหลักฐานใน passage: “concerned groups argue that enforcement remains necessary because illegal nets may still enter the vaquita's habitat” strongly believe = argue และ is necessary = remains necessary คำนี้พอดีกับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคและเป็นคำเดียวตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY

Paraphrased Vocabulary: concerned groups strongly believe = concerned groups argue; illegal nets may still enter = illegal nets may still enter; answer signal = enforcement
